item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images used in this article were created by the author for the article, and thus are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instead of saying reddit.com, just keep it as a more vague "various internet forums" or something to that effect. Like the very first comment here said, it feels out of place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Final boss defeated. Unknown - outcome yet to be achieved.

I'd say "put Dr. Bright on this", but given the implied assent to godhood…

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so here we have a TotleighSoft skip. Since this GoI still lacks a hub to link to, having a mention of the company name in-line link to another TotleighSoft skip would serve as a good way to bridge this article with its peers, as well as facilitate a wikiwalk of the TotleighSoft skips by those interested. I'm choosing SCP-2803 for this, since its a skip about the corporations HQ, and also has several links within it. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-2219 is an unlicensed Game Boy game titled "PORRIDGE" created by the Totleigh Software Corporation,1 where the main character "GOLDOX" must progress through levels and defeat bear-themed enemies. Apparently designed as a platforming-style adventure revolving around the story of "Goldilocks and the Three Bears", SCP-2219 comes without clear instructions and is subject to ambiguous gameplay goals and various glitches.

To this:

SCP-2219 is an unlicensed Game Boy game titled "PORRIDGE" created by the Totleigh Software Corporation,2 where the main character "GOLDOX" must progress through levels and defeat bear-themed enemies. Apparently designed as a platforming-style adventure revolving around the story of "Goldilocks and the Three Bears", SCP-2219 comes without clear instructions and is subject to ambiguous gameplay goals and various glitches.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Stylized as TotleighSoft
2. Stylized as TotleighSoft

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do the negative effects still apply if played by the remote analogue proxy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to see this turned into a real game, if only because of BÄRMILIPEDE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe they'd use "Hansen's disease", but put "leprosy" in parentheses after? That way, those not familiar with the medically standard term will know what that means. I mean, I've always known leprosy to be the more widespread term, but I could be wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
leprosy

Wouldn't the Foundation use "Hansen's disease"? It is generally considered more clinically correct, at least in the US.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GOLDOX is come to BÄRHAUS. Danger muchly.

Upvoted.

Honestly, I think I would've died of laughter reading the game description before the game ever got the opportunity to actually kill me (or otherwise maim me in some horrific way).

In all seriousness though, I liked this. The concept isn't necessarily new but the execution is phenomenal. Just enough comedy to be entertaining, plenty of enigmatic weirdness to make it provoking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dave answered that one earlier in the thread:

On an emulator - Won't have the anomalous effects, which come from the hardware itself.
With a Game Genie - You don't even wanna know.

So apparently no cheating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: could the foundation hack the game and beat it like that?

item.title: 500 PoRIGIE IE I AH H H H HH H H
item.content:encoded: I like it and it's backstory. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That new pic - dammit man, I can't upvote twice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh shit, everybody get ear protecton stat. Containment breach active. Sedative dart, now.

Uh… Hey little guy! Whatcha doin' out of your habitat? You want some stranglefruits?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cack?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just because they're intentionally not very unique or interesting doesn't make it okay that they're not very unique or interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since both Massy and this are from the Same (not yet) GoI it kinda makes sense. Also SCP-2803 sums it up good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Anomalous bootleg video game" seems to be redone from Massy a bit, but I like this article better than Massy. Still, it doesn't deviate from standard enough to justify an upvote (although the interview felt like a step in the right direction).

EDIT: Upon reading SCP-2803, my opinion of this article has been greatly tarnished to the point that I have to downvote. Refer to my post on the first page of 2803's discussion for my reasoning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
WHAT? No omnipotent reality-bender?
Sorry.
Thank you, Dave! ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't wait until that happens. This Skip was amazing but BRD willing someone will beat it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with minmin, also found it better before the edit, I found it stretched the disbeleif (Or something along those lines, I am terrible with this type of terminology) too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it better without that edit, but you can't please everyone.

Still upvote for BÄR.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As "videro gaem whut kilz ya" skips go, this one's pretty standard, though the positive effects are a nice touch.

But I had to upvote at "PROVE YOU WROTH!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You bring up a serious point regarding the psychiatric/psychological difference. I'mma go ahead and change that. Thanks for catching it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, pretty major issue:

Player experiences a sense of induced relaxation and contentment, consistent with heavy psychological medication.

Your terminology is way off. First, it's psychiatric medication, not psychological. Second, your description is vague, and only fits a few types of drugs. What you're describing here is pretty much straight-up opioids, or possibly anxiolytics/sedatives. For the second category think barbituates or benzodiazipines(Valium, Klonopin, Xannax, Rohypnol), basically anything with a street value.

Antipsychotics, antidepressents, and other types of psych meds don't work like this, particularly in high doses.

As for the article… I enjoyed the warped Goldilocks references, the "manual", and the implications of interview, but the middle log came off as a little too generic TTKY to keep my interest. This also hews very closely to the waters you charted with Massy, and I don't see a whole lot of improvement or new territory fleshed out in this one. (I mean, sure, you have it affecting the player and having some kind of incredible win state, but those are pretty generic tropes that don't add much to the story.)

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see the developers of Massy's Big Chance are still in the gaming market.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, I feel like that would get into SCP-WTF-J territory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of the whole article, I think I liked that part the best. More All EULAs should be written like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A words of CAUSHING: 5D feature doing much, failure is testamint to an WEAK UNDESERVING sort LEPERKIND. INCOMPETENCE will be PENALIZED this is necessary. You AGREE. Good.

Nice to see a EULA that gets right to the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FINE I'LL GIVE IN! I do like the addition of what the winner possibly gets. (Sometimes being vague isn't the best way to go.) I saw some of your drafts and I'm liking what I'm seeing from you! (You really good with E.Ts and E.T. related stuff!

+1 you son of a gun

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit, this a very well written SCP very interesting spin on a classic story and a very classic gaming system
Upvoted for creativity

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always try to reflect on how it'll look a few years down the line when Reddit is like Myspace… Doesn't detract from it, so why not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there should actually be a quick line about this in the article. They obviously would have tried it, so it's reasonable to include something along the lines of "[REDACTED] colon [REDACTED] live octopus [REDACTED] bees. Under no circumstance is this to be repeated."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On an emulator - Won't have the anomalous effects, which come from the hardware itself.
With a Game Genie - You don't even wanna know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you try to dump the ROM and play this game on an emulator? Or if you use a cheat device, such as a Game Genie?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given there's way too many "creepypasta" games out there, the Foundation barely needs to do anything; chances are the internet will get bored with this in a week for lack of any "hyper-realistic eyeballs".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps more could be written about the game itself like the levels and stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like I said in the critique forums, I wonder about your mind sometimes, Dave.
And that's coming from a guy who paints nightmarish monsters for fun. X3

Still think the bears would improve it, but +1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it resolved the issue I have with this, but I had upvoted already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a tiny line in the interview to add some clarity about what happens if you finish the game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My comments, version 1:
There. See what I mean? When you don't overdo it, you can write PERFECT SCP articles.
Wait wait wait, isn't 'perfect' a bit overdoing it?
No it isn't. PERFECT.

Except…

My comments, version 2:
THAT IS NOT FAIR
YOU CANNOT DO THAT TO ME, DAVE. YOU CANNOT.
WHAT HAPPENS IF YOU FINISH THE GAME????? I WANT TO KNOOOOOOOOOOOOOW!!!!!!!! BWAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAH! not nice.

Mmh. I wonder if overdoing it is infectious.

Okay. Off to upvote now. ;-D

ETA yeah, you just broke the 2219 curse. You should try 2214 next! rrh rrh rrh

ETA 2 oh shit, 2214 is the fart jokes SCP… Sorry!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like this article for its strong central image, but it doesn't quite explain enough to satisfy me. I'm left frustrated, rather than intrigued, by the obscurity of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, a pretty good SCP with great description, clinical tone, use of a table, and the interview feels very human, unlike many interview articles I seem to stumble upon. Hell, though simple, containment is described really well too. What cuts it short for me is the simplicity in it, like an anomalous game that gives you effects when played. It feels done before, but what keeps it out of Danger Zone is the intrigue in the mystery behind beating the game, implied by the player as well. Great overall, but for me personally, it would need to be a tad bit more interesting for me to really enjoy.

Neutral vote for now. Great job so far and good luck in the future!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Depress START to Levesque.

In other words… it's time to play the game?

I upvoted for the sake of that pun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fix'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure if it's my english thats off but shouldnt the defeat in "must progress through levels defeat bear-themed enemies." be "defeating" or "and defeat"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually liked the cliffhanger, it left an ambiguous element IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only part I disagree with is the interview (which left it on a cliffhanger), but I can accept the rest enough to give an upvote of confidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder what the foundations response would be, would they delete the post describing the game or would they pass it off as just a video game creepypasta.

It is nice to see totleighsoft again and I found the concept to be interesting though I feel more could be done with it. +1 though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing with Decibelles. I think it's fine the way it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes sense in the context of this article to be honest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You may have a point there. I'll see what I can do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that directly mentioning a website like Reddit isn't something done, it felt a bit out of place but it could just be me.
